@eagereyes I think that a web-native approach to journalism is needed. Maps/timelines/photos/videos and context + live updates.
@eagereyes I like @qz a lot as well. Biggest hole I see is breaking news, I think. Talked about it a bit here: https://t.co/Hm8Lqp5Q4O
@eagereyes The problem is figuring out what the MVP for a news organization is. Is it covering every important issue? How much coverage?
@eagereyes Minimal Viable Product http://t.co/9xtBEsGp50
NAMING THINGS IS SO HARD
Me to cop: "Which way do you want me to go?" Cop: "Which way do you want to go?" Me: "Which way won't get me beat?" Cop: pointed silence
With all love and respect, it's kind of a crazy world where National Public Radio has one of the best online graphics graphics departments.
Entire organizations shouldn't adhere to one medium or another- they should decide based on the individual story the best way to cover it.
Naming in news is indicative of it's legacy: organizations group themselves by medium, paper, TV, "online". Not how the world works anymore.
Field audio recording everything is one of the better obsessions I've had in recent years

Found an old SD card in my bedroom, throw it in my computer, it's a bunch of audio from #OccupyOakland.
Really, seriously playing around with the idea of starting my own news site (even if just an MVP) and getting excited/inspired.
